TextENGNew: ``Yo.@k@vS06/06/150602268. Everyone still alive?``
changeReason: New uses casual 'Yo' matching JPN よ, vs old's more polite 'Hey, everyone.'

TextENGNew: Kimiyoshi stared up at the ceiling, not moving a muscle.@k He might be dead.
changeReason: Old adds 'hanging from the collar' and 'when I walked in' not in JPN. New is faithful: staring at ceiling, not moving.

TextENGNew: Satoko was strapped to a restraining table, glaring at me through a face swollen red from crying.
changeReason: Old adds 'cross-shaped' not in JPN 拘束台 (restraining table).

TextENGNew: I had actually wanted to strap her to the special restraining table designed for hammering nails into the hands, but it was too large for Satoko.
I walked up to Kimiyoshi to check on him.@k ...No response.
changeReason: Old adds 'poked his body with my toe' not in JPN, which just says 近付いて見る (went closer to look).

TextENGNew: ``Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan, are you dead?``
changeReason: New preserves JPN 公由のおじいちゃん as 'Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan' (grandpa), while old used 'Uncle' (different relationship).

TextENGNew: ``...Murderer... murderer...!``
Judging by Satoko's state, she may have witnessed Kimiyoshi's death throes as he writhed and perished.
changeReason: New captures JPN 悶え死ぬ断末魔 (writhing death throes) which old reduced to 'last moments.'

TextENGNew: ``Final warning.@k@vS06/06/150602274. Are you alive?@k@vS06/06/150602275. If you're alive, answer me.``
changeReason: New translates JPN 最終通告 as 'Final warning' (direct match) vs old's 'This is your last chance.'

TextENGNew: ...There was no reply.
``Why are you doing this... Shion-san...!``
The door to the great cavern with the stone cells had been left wide open.
changeReason: New specifies 'the great cavern with the stone cells' matching JPN 岩牢のある大空洞, while old said 'the huge cave.'

TextENGNew: She must have been talking with the one in the cell while I was away.
So that was how she learned my name.@k Though that name was wrong now.

``I'm not Shion. I'm the real Mion.@k@vS06/06/150602279. The one you all thought was Mion is actually Shion.@k@vS06/06/150602280. I'm sure it's confusing, so you don't need to understand.@k@vS06/06/150602281. ...Heh heh heh...``
``...How dare you... everyone... Rika......!!``
changeReason: New includes 'everyone' matching JPN みんなを, while old only mentioned Rika.

TextENGNew: ``Rika Furude got what she deserved.@k@vS06/06/150602283. She came to kill me.@k@vS06/06/150602284. I simply turned the tables on her.``
``There's no way that's true!!!@k@vS06/04/150400038. Rika would never do such a thing!!``
``Believe what you want.@k@vS06/06/150602286. It's irrelevant to you anyway.``
``...What... are you going to do with me...?``
``Kill you.``
changeReason: New uses blunt 'Kill you' matching terse JPN 殺す, while old expanded to 'I'm going to kill you.'

TextENGNew: Satoko's eyes went wide. She must have had some inkling, but her face showed genuine shock nonetheless.
She surely hadn't expected me to state it so bluntly.
From the direction of the stone cells, I heard the clanging of iron bars.
Even echoing through the great cavern, Shion's voice came through clearly.
``Sis, nooooo!!@k@vS06/03/150300411. Don't kill Satoko!!``
I shouted back from where I stood.
``Ahahahahahaha!!!@k@vS06/06/150602288. Did you finally realize your sin?!@k@vS06/06/150602289. You never did this for Satoshi-kun!!@k@vS06/06/150602290. That's why he was killed like an insect!!``
``You ignored ten billion moments when you could have saved Satoshi-kun--you abandoned him!!@k@vS06/06/150602292. Yes, THAT is your sin!!!``
changeReason: New uses 'ten billion moments' matching JPN 百億, while old reduced to 'millions.'

TextENGNew: ``Gyaaaaahhh!!!``
Tears streamed down Satoko's face as she clenched her teeth so hard they looked ready to crack.
``Stoooop, stop it!!@k@vS06/03/150300413. Don't kill Satoko!!@k@vS06/03/150300414. Forgive her!!@k@vS06/03/150300415. I don't care what happens to me... but please, forgive Satoko!!``

Why couldn't you say that for Satoshi-kun?!@k It's too late--far too late!!!@k Yes, the one screaming right now is the me from back then.@k No matter how loudly you scream, it won't reach anyone, it won't spare him, it won't save him!!
Go on and taste the agony of being unable to save someone!!!
changeReason: New captures JPN 救えない苦しみを味わうがいい more precisely than old's general 'go through the same thing.'

TextENGNew: ``Stop, Mion, stop, Sis... stop stop stop...!!!``

``All right, Shion.@k@vS06/06/150602294. Let's do this: I'll tell you how to save Satoko.@k@vS06/06/150602295. You're going to say 'I'm sorry' one thousand times.@k@vS06/06/150602296. When you finish, I'll set Satoko free.``

``Well?!@k@vS06/06/150602298. I don't lie. If you count to one thousand, I will absolutely keep my promise.@k@vS06/06/150602299. Think of it as your atonement.@k@vS06/06/150602300. Now then!!@k@vS06/06/150602301. What will it be?!``
``I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sor...``
Shion began repeating her apology like a broken cassette tape.
At times like these, she was stupidly earnest.
It never even crossed her mind to fudge the count.
She would undoubtedly count all the way to one thousand, properly and faithfully.
``Waaaahhhh, waaaahhhh!!! WAAAAHHHHH!!!!``
Satoko screamed...
As if trying to drown out that scream, Shion's voice--'I'm sorry, I'm sorry'--grew louder...

``Ahahahaha!@k@vS06/06/150602303. Crying is your specialty, isn't it?@k@vS06/06/150602304. Think Nii-nii will come running if you cry?@k@vS06/06/150602305. Think he'll rush in, knock me aside, and rescue you?!``
``Satoko, can you hear me?@k@vS06/06/150602307. Are you listening?@k@vS06/06/150602308. I said LISTEN!!!``
I seized Satoko's face and forced her to look at me.
changeReason: Old says 'slapped her face' but JPN says ぐいっと掴み (seized/grabbed); new correctly uses 'seized.'

TextENGNew: ``Do you understand what you've done?@k@vS06/06/150602310. ...Do you know how much your very existence drove Satoshi-kun into a corner?``
``...Nii-nii... Nii-nii...``

``That's right--all you ever had to do was cry for Nii-nii.@k@vS06/06/150602312. Every time you cried, Satoshi-kun would shield you.@k@vS06/06/150602313. But have you ever once considered what kind of burden you were to him?``
``Satoshi-kun wasn't your hero, and he wasn't invincible.@k@vS06/06/150602315. He was the same as you!!!``
``When hurt, he bled. When in pain, he groaned. A human being, exactly like you!!``
changeReason: New captures both JPN elements: 'neither your hero nor invincible,' while old only said 'wasn't some superhero.'

TextENGNew: ``Did you ever understand the pain Satoshi-kun endured, standing in the line of fire in your place?!``
changeReason: New adds 'standing in the line of fire in your place' matching JPN 矢面に立ってくれた, which old omitted.

TextENGNew: ``You didn't!!``
``You never even tried!!!``
``You clung to Satoshi-kun!!@k@vS06/06/150602321. You were a parasite!!@k@vS06/06/150602322. Yes--you survived by licking the blood that flowed from Satoshi-kun's wounds!!``
``Bearing all that curse upon you, and you STILL have the gall to call Satoshi-kun's name?!!@k@vS06/06/150602324. Well?!?!``

``...Hah... haah, haha, ha!@k@vS06/06/150602326. You will now atone for your sin.@k@vS06/06/150602327. By my hand.@k@vS06/06/150602328. Cry and scream as much as you like.@k@vS06/06/150602329. And learn that no matter how much you cry, Satoshi-kun will never come to save you.``
``Yes--you drove him into the corner, you made him vanish, YOU killed Satoshi-kun!!!``
Hah!@k Hah!@k Hah!!
Having spewed everything from the pit of my stomach in one breath, I exhaled raggedly.
But Satoko's reaction was disappointing.
The crying and screaming from moments ago had suddenly subsided.

``...What's the matter?@k@vS06/06/150602335. Holding back your screams as a form of resistance, thinking it'd please me too much?@k@vS06/06/150602336. Heh heh heh!!@k@vS06/06/150602337. Good for you, ahahahahaha!!!``
My crazed laughter didn't faze Satoko at all. She looked at me with gritted teeth and resolve in her eyes.
changeReason: 'Resignation' vs 'resolve' differ in nuance but both describe Satoko's determined response to Shion.

TextENGNew: ``Yes... I... have always thought so too.``
changeReason: New captures 'have always thought so too' matching JPN そう思ってましたのよ, vs old's present tense 'I think that's the truth.'

TextENGNew: ``I drove Nii-nii into a corner. ...That's why he disappeared.@k@vS06/04/150400055. ...I realized that the very day Nii-nii vanished...``
``...I... was not strong enough.@k@vS06/04/150400057. ...I depended on Nii-nii too much.@k@vS06/04/150400058. ...Nii-nii must have been suffering too, and yet... I couldn't stop clinging to him.``
``...On that point... you are absolutely right.@k@vS06/04/150400060. I have... no excuse.``
Satoko's tone carried neither sarcasm nor any attempt to curry my favor.@k ...It was completely natural.
``...That's... unexpected.@k@vS06/06/150602341. I didn't realize you were aware of your own sin.``
changeReason: New uses 'unexpected' matching JPN 意外, while old said 'interesting' (different reaction).

TextENGNew: ``Nii-nii will definitely come back.@k@vS06/04/150400062. Someday, he'll surely return...``

``But when he does... I'm going to show him how grown-up I've become...@k@vS06/04/150400064. I won't depend on Nii-nii anymore.@k@vS06/04/150400065. I won't hide behind him anymore.@k@vS06/04/150400066. ...I'm going to show him... that I can stand on my own...``
changeReason: New uses 'grown-up' matching JPN 一人前, while old used 'independent' and 'I don't need him anymore' distorting the growth nuance.

TextENGNew: ``Haha... hahaha!!@k@vS06/06/150602343. 'When he comes back,' she says!!!``
But Satoko didn't let out a pitiful scream.
She gritted her teeth and swallowed the pain.

``Nii-nii... will definitely come back.@k@vS06/04/150400068. Absolutely, without a doubt, he'll come back...!!@k@vS06/04/150400069. I'm waiting for him!!@k@vS06/04/150400070. And when he does, I'll apologize for depending on him for so long...``
changeReason: New emphasizes JPN 絶対に絶対に with 'Absolutely, without a doubt,' while old compressed to 'I know he is.'

TextENGNew: ``Until then... I will not lose... I will not be defeated...!!@k@vS06/04/150400072. I will never depend on Nii-nii again!!``

``No matter what hardship I face, I will not call for Nii-nii!!@k@vS06/04/150400074. I won't call! I won't cry! I won't scream!!@k@vS06/04/150400075. If I cry, Nii-nii will come.@k@vS06/04/150400076. So I will endure this by myself!!!``
``D-...Don't you dare talk down to me, you little brat!!!``
changeReason: New captures JPN 上等抜かすな小娘 more accurately with 'Don't you dare talk down to me, you little brat.'

TextENGNew: ``If tormenting me is what pleases you, then by all means, have at it!!!@k@vS06/04/150400078. But if you're expecting me to cry out for Nii-nii, then I'm sorry to disappoint.``
changeReason: Old adds 'stab me' not present in JPN いじめて楽しいなら (if tormenting me is fun).

TextENGNew: ``I will not cry--absolutely, absolutely, ABSOLUTELY NOT!!!@k@vS06/04/150400080. If you doubt me, then test me all you like!!!@k@vS06/04/150400081. Nii-nii is watching me. He's watching me endure this, watching me prove I've truly grown.``
``He'll see that I'm not a crybaby anymore!!!@k@vS06/04/150400083. And when Nii-nii sees that, he'll come back!!!``
``Go ahead, test me all you want!!@k@vS06/04/150400085. I will not cry, I will not shed a single tear!!!@k@vS06/04/150400086. See for yourself how strong Satoko Houjou is!!!``
Damn, damn, damn...!!!
I'll shut that insolent mouth with pain and screams!!
Take this, and this, and this!!
``Nii-nii, you're watching, aren't you? You can see me, can't you?@k@vS06/04/150400088. ...Look how patient I've become...@k@vS06/04/150400089. Nothing can break me anymore...``

``This much,@k@vS06/04/150400091. this little,@k@vS06/04/150400092. something like this--@k@vS06/04/150400093. ...I won't cry.@k@vS06/04/150400094. I won't call for help.@k@vS06/04/150400095. I can endure it.@k@vS06/04/150400096. I will endure it.@k@vS06/04/150400097. This is nothing compared to the pain Nii-nii bore in my place!!``
Damn damn damn damn!!!
Why doesn't she feel the pain--or is what I'm doing really not painful enough?!
changeReason: Both ask why she doesn't feel pain; 'Doesn't it hurt?' vs 'is what I'm doing not painful enough?' are similar frustration.

TextENGNew: That's a lie, impossible!!@k How can she still talk back? Why won't she cry or scream?!!
Once Satoko had said everything she wanted to say, she clenched her jaw tight and said nothing more.
No matter what I did, she wouldn't even let me hear her grinding her teeth.
What on earth was this girl?@k This wasn't the Satoko I knew.@k I'd never seen anyone this strong.
changeReason: Old's 'didn't need anymore' implies rejection; new's 'no longer depending on' matches JPN 甘えない (growth, not rejection).

TextENGNew: ...If Satoko had been this strong, she wouldn't have been a burden on Satoshi-kun.
Why hadn't she possessed this strength before?
Too late, too late--everything was too late.
This fool's enlightenment had come far, far too late...!!
Satoko continued to bite down on her agony, enduring it without letting it show.
Even under treatment cruel enough to drive the observer mad... as if displaying her own indomitable strength...
I couldn't defeat Satoko.
I couldn't break her strength.
Satoko had... become what she aspired to be.
...A person who stood on her own, no longer depending on Satoshi-kun.
She had been waiting all this time, believing Satoshi-kun would come back.
She had been forging herself, tempering her heart, so that when her brother returned someday, she could show him how strong she had become.
changeReason: New captures JPN 自らを心を鍛えてきた (forged/tempered herself), while old only said 'looks forward to showing him.'

TextENGNew: And the one who confirmed it was not Satoshi-kun, but... me--who never believed for an instant that Satoshi-kun would return, who never even considered waiting for him.
What... was I?
I decided Satoshi-kun was dead.@k I assumed it as fact.
Why had I assumed that...?
...Because I had no intention of waiting.
I didn't believe Satoshi-kun would come back.
I didn't wait.
...Pursuing vengeance for Satoshi-kun... in the end, that was just another way of trying to forget him.
...But Satoko was different.
She never assumed he was dead.
No--maybe she had, just a little.
But even so, she waited.
Wanting to show the returning Satoshi-kun how she had been reborn, she waited... and waited... and waited...
And what about me?!
I did nothing.
I merely wanted an enemy to distract me from my grief.
Using vengeance for Satoshi-kun as justification, I became a demon--all because I wanted to forget my human heart.
I lost.
Unable to endure the pain of waiting for Satoshi-kun, I lost, and chose the path of discarding my human heart.
Which meant I had also discarded the heart that loved Satoshi-kun.
...Yes--it was no different from discarding Satoshi-kun himself.
Satoko won.
Her mouth, which had been clenched so tightly, had relaxed, looking at peace.
Through half-closed eyelids, her eyes peeked out faintly.
Those eyes looked as though she were dozing off while dreaming a pleasant dream.
And within those eyes... Satoshi-kun was unmistakably there.
Satoko had... held on... until Satoshi-kun came to welcome her...
Satoko ran toward Satoshi-kun.
And she leaped into his arms.
Satoshi-kun... held her tight.@k ...And they spun round and round.
You can cry now.
...When Satoshi-kun said that softly, Satoko's face crumpled... and she wailed.
Satoshi-kun stroked the head of the sobbing, clinging Satoko, gently... so gently.
...From now on, we'll be together always.@k ...Gently... so gently.
Satoshi-kun...... I was waiting for you too...
What hollow words...
I had long since killed the Satoshi-kun inside my heart with my own hands.
changeReason: New adds 'with my own hands' matching JPN 自らの手で, emphasizing Shion's agency.

TextENGNew: ...I never believed for a moment in our reunion.
...Seeing Satoko alone reunited with Satoshi-kun,@k ...and only now mouthing such self-serving words...!
As Satoshi-kun continued to hold Satoko, our eyes met.
Shion.
Satoshi-kun said.
...I asked you to do something, didn't I?
Eh... asked me...?
I racked my memory desperately.
Had Satoshi-kun ever asked me for anything...?
Satoshi-kun watched me with such sadness... because I had forgotten that precious promise...
...His lonely smile cut through my chest more sharply than any blade.
changeReason: Old fabricates 'like vomit with filthy chunks' not in JPN which says 濁りきったヘドロやゴミ (muddy sludge and refuse).

TextENGNew: What flowed from the wound wasn't beautiful blood--it was a thick, filthy sludge mixed with rot and refuse.
...That was the true nature of what filled me.
``...Whether it's the curse, or some conspiracy by someone in this village.@k@vS06/07/151200325. ...I will absolutely not disappear. I refuse to be erased.``
``Y-You won't disappear, Satoshi-kun...``

``I won't.@k@vS06/07/151200327. ...Not until I buy that stuffed animal for Satoko.@k@vS06/07/151200328. I'll be getting my pay soon.@k@vS06/07/151200329. Until that day, I absolutely won't disappear.``
...Huh?
This is... a memory from when...?
...That's right.@k This was the phone call from Satoshi-kun, the day before last year's Watanagashi.
``...Ah... my aunt is home. I have to hang up now.``
His aunt had come home, and he surely wanted to hang up as quickly as possible.
...Satoshi-kun paused for a single breath, then spoke as if delivering the most important words he had.
changeReason: New captures JPN 一番大事な一言を告げるように (as if delivering most important words), which old omitted.

TextENGNew: ``Take care of Satoko for me...... okay?``
changeReason: New translates JPN 頼むからね as 'Take care of Satoko for me, okay?' while old's 'I'll leave Satoko to you' is more detached.

TextENGNew: My knees and elbows began to tremble.
My molars clattered against each other, and my fingertips shook.
I... Satoshi-kun... asked me to......@k Wh... waaahhh... ahhh... waaaaahhhhh...
What... have I done...?
I couldn't wait for Satoshi-kun to return,@k so I filled my heart with a demon and did as I pleased.
I didn't believe Satoshi-kun would come back,@k ...and Satoshi-kun, who had placed his trust in me... his very last, his only request... I had forgotten.
How could someone like me claim to love Satoshi-kun...?
I wanted to forget the grief of losing Satoshi-kun, so I drenched myself in blood. Looking like this, how could I ever welcome him back?
I didn't believe Satoshi-kun would return.
I didn't believe Satoshi-kun was alive.
Because I didn't believe he was alive, I decided he was dead.
Yes--I chose to believe in Satoshi-kun's death.
I killed Satoshi-kun inside my heart.
No one else... I was the one who killed him.
But Satoko believed until the very end.
And... her faith was rewarded in her final moment.
Satoko was no longer here.
I was still here.
Trapped in this defiled, blood-soaked flesh.@k Rotting away inside this prison of meat.
changeReason: New captures JPN 肉の牢 (prison of flesh/meat) which old omitted.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun... had been waiting for Satoko, and for me too.
But to be reunited with Satoshi-kun, we had to keep our promises.
Satoko's promise... was to become strong.
Satoko kept her promise to the very end.
...And Satoshi-kun embraced her.
My promise... was to take care of Satoko in Satoshi-kun's place.
I had never kept it. Not from the very start.
That was why... Satoshi-kun... would never come to embrace me.
Even if I were to stroke Satoko's wounds now, murmuring 'does it hurt...?'@k it would mean nothing.
At that moment.@k@|@y ...I felt a presence behind me.
Standing there expressionlessly, it was neither Satoshi-kun nor the eerie presence that had frightened me before.
I didn't know who it was.
...It simply stood there in silence... wordlessly accusing me of my mistakes.
...Yes.@k ...At last, I understood.@k That... was me.
I had been standing far behind myself the entire time, watching myself break the promise with my own hands.
Satoshi-kun had entrusted Satoko to me... yet I had torn that promise apart before my own eyes, and I had been watching the whole time.
...That other me wept... and gave up on even this me...
Wait... Satoshi-kun has given up on me... and now even I myself have given up on me... what happens to me then...?
I don't want to be in this darkness... I don't want to be in this filthy, blood-drenched place...
Don't leave me all alone...
If I'm abandoned by my own self, what becomes of... this body...?
Nothing... will remain...!!!
All that's left is the thick, oozing demon filling the bottom of my heart!
Ah... so that's what I am.
...I already knew it, didn't I?@k ...That I'm... nothing but a demon.
``Wahaha, hahahahahahaha... ahahahahahaha!!!``
A demon has no heart to feel sadness.
A demon's heart knows no pain.
A demon... has no need for tears...!
The fool Shion was still faithfully repeating her apology, like clockwork.
changeReason: New captures JPN まぬけ (fool) and 律儀に (faithfully) which old omitted.

TextENGNew: ...Believing steadfastly that it was the only way for her to save Satoko.
Her voice was nearly gone, reduced to a faint rasp.
``SHIIOOOON!!!@k@vS06/06/150602356. You don't need to count anymore. Good work.``
``...Waaaaahhhhhhh...``
She must have understood what that meant for Satoko.@k Shion's weeping echoed from the stone cell.
``Ahahahahahahaha!!@k@vS06/06/150602358. Being a demon isn't half bad!!``
``I've had more than my fill of fun in this human world.@k@vS06/06/150602361. ...Before long, I'll take my own life and return to the land of demons.``
``But don't worry.@k@vS06/06/150602363. I'll make sure to kill you first.@k@vS06/06/150602364. That's what I'm here for, after all.``
``...Oh right, you're in love with Kei-chan, aren't you?@k@vS06/06/150602367. All right then--I'll tear Kei-chan apart right before your eyes!!``

``Yes, Onigafuchi Village is a village of demons!!@k@vS06/06/150602371. A village with legends of demons emerging from the swamp to attack!!@k@vS06/06/150602372. I too shall wreak havoc like those legendary demons, then await Oyashiro-sama's judgment.``
``Now THAT would be a calamity worthy of Hinamizawa, wouldn't it?!!@k@vS06/06/150602374. Ahahahahahahahahahahaha!!!``
``You weren't answering, so I was just about to hang up, thinking you were out.``
``Ah... ah, sorry...@k@vS06/01/150100257. I was just, um... in the bath.``
``...Wouldn't a family member have picked up?@k@vS06/06/150602380. Could it be... are your parents not home right now, Kei-chan?``
``No, it's not that... they're not really away... A-A-Anyway!!@k@vS06/01/150100259. Have you heard?!@k@vS06/01/150100260. About Rika-chan and Satoko?``
``No.@k@vS06/06/150602382. ...What happened after that?@k@vS06/06/150602383. Were they found?``
``...Kei-chan, please don't lose heart...``
``.........Rika-chan... and Satoko... they were kidnapped, weren't they...``
``...Yes.@k@vS06/06/150602386. There's little doubt about that...``

``.........Rika-chan and Satoko were... ah, no... I mean... sorry.@k@vS06/01/150100265. ...Um... the mayor... after he was kidnapped... what do you think happened...?@k@vS06/01/150100266. Do you think... he's still alive...?``
There was hesitation in Keiichi's words.
I could tell he wanted to believe Satoko and Rika were still alive, which was why he was asking indirectly.
...Keiichi was the same as Satoko.
Unless the kidnapper was after ransom, the victim would normally be killed quickly.
If it were me, I wouldn't dream of them being alive.
...But Keiichi was pushing back against that terrifying conclusion, trying to cling to even the faintest hope that they still lived.
That quiet, unassuming strength of heart--right now, I could feel it painfully well.
changeReason: New captures JPN 目立たない心の強さが痛いくらいによくわかる fully, while old condensed to 'I could feel his strength.'

TextENGNew: ...And I understood that his strength far exceeded mine.
...I was a demon now, after all.@k ...I offered no excuses.
``I think they were killed.``
``...He... hey, wait... just like that...``

``...I think kidnapping someone and maintaining that state must be extremely difficult.@k@vS06/06/150602389. ...Unless they're being held as hostages, the most rational thing is to kill them once they've served their purpose.``
``K-K-Kill them... just like that...@k@vS06/01/150100269. D-Do you think human lives are worth nothing...?!?!``
changeReason: Old's 'what's a human life or two, anyway' sounds dismissive; JPN is angry 'Do you think human lives are worth nothing?!' New correctly conveys outrage.

TextENGNew: ``...It's a terrible thing, I know.@k@vS06/06/150602391. ...But that's probably what happened.``

``If the mayor was killed... then...@k@vS06/01/150100271. does that mean Rika-chan and Satoko... might have been killed the same way...?!?!@k@vS06/01/150100272. ...N-No... that can't... they're not just some random old man, you know?!``
``We're talking about Rika-chan!@k@vS06/01/150100274. We're talking about Satoko!@k@vS06/01/150100275. Th-They couldn't... they couldn't just kill them that easily......!``
Even hearing the word 'killed' directly from my mouth, Keiichi still resisted.
He fought desperately not to kill the friends inside his heart.
...I hadn't fought.
I had killed him so easily.
I blamed Shion for not protecting Satoshi-kun, and yet... I myself had killed Satoshi-kun within me without a second thought.
``It's probably better to assume that Rika-chan and Satoko-chan... met the same fate.``
``WAAAAAHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH...!!!!``
Keiichi... howled.@k ...And wept.
It was as though the death throes of Satoko and Rika within his heart were his own.

``Why?!@k@vS06/01/150100278. Why kidnap and kill people...?!?!@k@vS06/01/150100279. I don't care about curses! Are human lives really that cheap?!@k@vS06/01/150100280. Erasing and killing them without a second thought!!``
``...You're right.@k@vS06/06/150602394. ...Absolutely right.@k@vS06/06/150602395. ...Only a demon could do such a thing...``
Hahahaha...
I laughed at myself.@k He surely couldn't hear it through the receiver.
``Hey, Shion.@k@vS06/01/150100282. ...Tell me.@k@vS06/01/150100283. ...Why did Hinamizawa end up as a place where people have to die on the night of Watanagashi?``
``...That's what I'd like to know.``

``The fight over the dam project is already over!@k@vS06/01/150100285. Haven't enough people been cursed already?!@k@vS06/01/150100286. Why? Why?!@k@vS06/01/150100287. If there's a reason, then say it!!!``
``...I understand how you feel, Kei-chan, I truly do.@k@vS06/06/150602398. ...The dam conflict ended ages ago.``
``And yet every year, murders and disappearances keep repeating under the name of Oyashiro-sama's Curse.@k@vS06/06/150602400. ...Why?!@k@vS06/06/150602401. Why does it keep happening?!``
``Some of them had nothing to do with it, some didn't deserve to be cursed at all--so why?@k@vS06/06/150602403. ...WHY?!?!``
``That's what I'M asking YOUUUU!!!!``
``And I want to know just as badly as you do!!!!!``
Yes.@k Oyashiro-sama's Curse never existed from the beginning.
There was merely a bizarre environment where murder could be committed and excused under the name of Oyashiro-sama.
But that was nothing more than fertile soil.
Without demons willing to dance upon it, it was meaningless.
``Listen.@k@vS06/06/150602406. ...Something as unrealistic as Oyashiro-sama's curse never existed in the first place.``

``Just as you say, Kei-chan--someone in the village has been using the convenient justification of Oyashiro-sama's Curse@k@vS06/06/150602408. to repeat murders and disappearances with every Watanagashi!!!@k@vS06/06/150602409. ...They were cunning.``
``They exploited the curse to build a system that eliminated two of the village's enemies every year.@k@vS06/06/150602411. ...And this year, that system killed Takano-san and the others!!``
changeReason: Old specifies 'Takano-san and Tomitake-san' while JPN 鷹野さんたちが says 'Takano-san and the others'; new is more faithful.

TextENGNew: ``Then two should have been enough!!@k@vS06/01/150100290. Why did the mayor and Rika-chan and Satoko have to be killed too?!@k@vS06/01/150100291. And I don't give a damn about the past!!``
``The only thing I care about is why Rika-chan and Satoko had to die!!@k@vS06/01/150100293. Why?!@k@vS06/01/150100294. I didn't even tell Satoko anything!!@k@vS06/01/150100295. Why even Satoko...``
``WHYYYYYYYY?!?!``
Those were Satoshi-kun's words, spoken through Keiichi's mouth.
What could I possibly say in response...?
Nothing.
I could only stand there in silence, receiving those blade-like words in my chest.

``.........Kei-chan... please... stop being so harsh...@k@vS06/06/150602413. ...hic...@k@vS06/06/150602414. .........I don't understand what's happening either...@k@vS06/06/150602415. Everything changed... since that night we snuck into the ritual storehouse...``

``...When I heard that Takano-san and the others died that night... I couldn't believe my ears.@k@vS06/06/150602417. ...And then... I realized immediately.@k@vS06/06/150602418. ...That now... it was my turn to be targeted...``

``Four of us broke in.@k@vS06/06/150602420. ...There's no way just Kei-chan and I would be conveniently spared...@k@vS06/06/150602421. ...When I realized that, you can imagine how alone I felt... how terrified... Kei-chan, you understand that feeling, don't you...?``

``...That's why I... confided in Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan...@k@vS06/06/150602423. ...He didn't get angry...@k@vS06/06/150602424. ...He smiled and said, if Shion-chan truly feels remorse, there's no way she'll be demoned away...``
``...He really... smiled... and said to leave it to him......@k@vS06/06/150602426. ...ooh...!!``
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan's words disparaging Satoshi-kun came flooding back...
I killed him, yes, but Hinamizawa killed him too. Everyone ganged up and killed Satoshi-kun.
I'm not the only one at fault. I'm not the only one...!
``............When... did you tell the mayor...?``

``.........I'm sorry... Shion.@k@vS06/01/150100300. ...I know I'm piling onto your grief.@k@vS06/01/150100301. ...But Shion, you... told him, right?@k@vS06/01/150100302. Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan. The mayor.``
Before I knew it, Keiichi's tone had become razor-sharp.
It was a cruel sharpness, but one befitting a verdict.
Not a dull blade that inflicts needless pain.@k ...A clean, almost pleasurable sharpness.
``...Did you know that the mayor had been suffering from severe hemorrhoids and was receiving treatment at a hospital...?``
``...Um... Kei-chan...?@k@vS06/06/150602432. ...What does that... have to do with...?``
``...Just answer me.@k@vS06/01/150100307. If you don't know, just say you don't know.``
``...I knew... about the hemorrhoids...@k@vS06/06/150602434. ...He always looked pained when sitting...``
``He was going to the hospital regularly.@k@vS06/01/150100309. ...You know which hospital, right?``
``...............I'm sorry, I don't know that much...@k@vS06/06/150602436. ...But, Kei-chan... what does this have to do with...``
``...I'll be direct.@k@vS06/01/150100311. ...Shion... when did you tell the mayor?@k@vS06/01/150100312. Where, and when?@k@vS06/01/150100313. ...A rough answer is fine... but please, tell me.``
``.........Kei-chan... why are you asking that...@k@vS06/06/150602438. ...hic...``
...Well done, Keiichi Maebara.@k ...You've seen through me.

``...It's impossible.@k@vS06/01/150100315. ...The day the mayor disappeared.@k@vS06/01/150100316. From morning until he vanished.@k@vS06/01/150100317. ...There was no opportunity for you to have contacted him.``

``The mayor left first thing that morning for a solo visit to a university hospital that nobody knew about.@k@vS06/01/150100319. ...Shion, you just said you didn't know which hospital.@k@vS06/01/150100320. ...So you couldn't have been in contact with the mayor until he returned.@k@vS06/01/150100321. ...Right?``
``His return train was delayed by an accident, so he didn't get home until right before the meeting was about to start.``
``...In other words, there was no time to confide in him before the meeting either.@k@vS06/01/150100324. ...Shion, did you attend the meeting...?``

``And did you confide in him during the meeting...?@k@vS06/01/150100326. Because if you didn't do it then... there's no time left.@k@vS06/01/150100327. ...After the meeting, the mayor disappeared on his way home.``
``.........Well, actually, the meeting, I...``

``That day, you didn't even show up to the part-time job you said you were going to.@k@vS06/01/150100329. ...Let me put it more plainly.@k@vS06/01/150100330. ...You haven't... been seen by anyone since we went to the library together.``
``...Shion Sonozaki...@k@|@y@vS06/01/150100332. ...disappeared the day after Watanagashi.``
Ahahaha... Bingo, Kei-chan.@k ...I have to admit, I'm impressed.
``...Ooishi-san says you've already disappeared.@k@vS06/01/150100334. ...And yet... you've been calling me every night, haven't you, Shion...?@k@vS06/01/150100335. ...Please, Shion.``
``...If anything I've said is wrong... tell me it's wrong.@k@vS06/01/150100337. Shion, I'm begging you...``
Corner me more...
Corner me so thoroughly that no matter how pitifully the demon inside me tries to make excuses, there's no escape.
``Shion, you never met with the mayor.@k@vS06/01/150100339. ...But if you did meet him......``
...If Shion had any opportunity to meet the mayor on that day... it would have been...
``After the mayor finished the meeting. ...In other words! ...Right before he disappeared, or else... after he'd already been taken!!!!``
CRASH!!
changeReason: New corrects 'Click!' to 'CRASH!!' matching JPN ガシャン (slamming sound).

TextENGNew: I slammed down the receiver, shrieking with laughter.
Brilliantly done, Kei-chan!!
That's right--it's me. I'm the one behind everything!
Hurry, corner me.@k Hurry... set me... free.
Ahahahaha, ahahahahahaha...!
Would Keiichi go to the police, or come here himself?@k I wondered which it would be...
If it was the police... well, I'd struggle as much as I could.
Let's see how skillfully Ooishi could capture a single demon.
And if Kei-chan came alone?
...That would be foolish.@k He couldn't complain about being devoured.
I fully intended to devour him.@k To drag him before Shion and kill him in the most gruesome way possible.
So Kei-chan... don't you dare show me mercy either.
I never thought I'd end up placing such expectations on someone I'd only considered 'a mildly entertaining guy'...
